notes.anchor: in view of our close tie
notes.note: The 'necessitudo' Cicero invokes with Cato is the bond of mutual political obligation; he writes privately to a leading senator the news he is, for the moment, withholding from an official dispatch.

notes.anchor: to whom the sister of the king of the Armenians is married
notes.note: The Parthian prince Pacorus was tied by marriage to the Armenian royal house (the sister of Artavasdes II), the dynastic link that made an Armenian attack on Cappadocia credible.
Cicero distinguishes his private letter to Cato from a 'publice' dispatch to the Senate, which he judged unnecessary since both Antiochus and the proconsul Bibulus would report directly.
The Ides fell on the 13th of August (the 13th in months other than March, May, July, and October); Cicero reckons Bibulus's sailing date from Ephesus by it.

notes.anchor: hold in affection and defend the absent
notes.note: A standard request to a powerful friend at Rome to look after a provincial governor's reputation and interests while he is away on his command.
